growth_records.agit: static (no shaking reported; Balch tubes and Pyrex side-arm flasks)
growth_records.cite: Greening et al. Diverse hydrogen production and consumption pathways influence methane production in ruminants. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0464-2. [PMC6776011]
growth_records.snippet: Growth yield (OD600) 0.36±0.01; Specific growth rate (h-1) 0.33±0.06 ... Fumarate (mM) -46.3, Succinate (mM) +54.6 [pure culture, 0-12 h]
growth_records.notes: Specific growth rate (mu=0.33 h-1) directly reported in Table 1 (pure culture of W. succinogenes DSM 1740, anaerobic modified Balch medium, headspace 20% CO2/80% H2 as electron donor for fumarate respiration, 37 degC). Fumarate uptake and succinate secretion CALCULATED as delta-concentration/12h (Table 1 footnote gives change between 0 and 12h): fumarate 46.3/12=3.858 mM/h; succinate 54.6/12=4.55 mM/h. Volumetric rates, not per-gDW, since no OD-to-biomass conversion given. Headspace H2 dropped -78.4% over 12h (gas-phase percent, not converted to a rate). Medium composition (Table S6) not reproduced in main text.

growth_records.agit: static (no shaking reported; Balch tubes and Pyrex side-arm flasks)
growth_records.medium: modified Balch medium (co-culture with Ruminococcus albus 7)
growth_records.cite: Greening et al. Diverse hydrogen production and consumption pathways influence methane production in ruminants. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0464-2. [PMC6776011]
growth_records.snippet: Specific growth rate (h-1) co-culture 0.57±0.34 (Ra)/0.54±0.11 (Ws) ... Fumarate (mM) -43.1, Succinate +55.4, Acetate +32.4, Ethanol +0.3, H2 BDL [co-culture, 0-12 h]
growth_records.notes: W. succinogenes growth rate in co-culture (mu=0.54 h-1) directly reported in Table 1, tracked by qPCR alongside R. albus. Metabolite concentration changes (Table 1, 0-12h) are for the COMBINED co-culture and cannot be attributed solely to W. succinogenes; rates calculated as delta-conc/12h (same values as paired R. albus co-culture record, see that record's notes). Volumetric (mM/h), not gDW-specific.
